subject: Physics
solution:
The given equation of the wave is $y = 5\cos\pi\left(200t - \dfrac{x}{150}\right)$

Rewriting the equation, we get $y = 5\cos\left(200\pi t - \dfrac{\pi x}{150}\right)$

Comparing this with the standard wave equation $y = A\cos(\omega t - kx)$, we have:

$\omega = 200\pi$ rad/s

$k = \dfrac{\pi}{150}$ rad/cm

The velocity of the wave is given by $v = \dfrac{\omega}{k}$

$v = \dfrac{200\pi}{\dfrac{\pi}{150}} = 30000$ cm/s

Converting to m/s, we get $v = \dfrac{30000}{100} = 300$ m/s

Answer: $300$

subject: Physics

For train B leaving the station, 

f1=300330-0330--30=275 Hz     ∵v0=0

For train A reaching the station, 

f2=300330-0330-30=330 Hz

The difference of frequencies heard by the person∆f=330-275=55 Hz

solution:
The formula to calculate the fundamental frequency produced in a vibrating string is given by

f0=v2L   ...1

For the first case, it can be written that

120=v2(90)    ...2

Similarly, for the second case, it can be written that

180=v2(L)    ...3

Divide equation (3) by equation (2) and solve to obtain the required length of the string.

180120=v2Lv290= 90L⇒L= 90×120180=60

subject: Physics
solution:
Frequency of nth harmonic is nv2l=400⇒nv0.6=400   ...1 

And frequency of n+1th harmonic is n+1v2l=450⇒n+1v0.6=450    ...2, here, v is speed of wave on string. 

From equation 1, we have

n=400×0.6v

Putting in equation 2, 

⇒0.6×400v+1v0.6=450

⇒400+v0.6=450

⇒v=30 m s-1

Speed of wave on string is stated as v=Tμ=30, here, μ is linear mass density.

So, μ=Tv2=2700900⇒μ=3 kg m-1

solution:
The formula to calculate the fundamental frequency in a closed pipe is given by

fc=v4l1   ...1

And, that in an open pipe is given by

fo=v2l2   ...2

Given that

fc-fo=7   ...3

From equations (1), (2) and (3), it follows that

v4×150-v2×350=7⇒v600 cm-v700 cm=7⇒v6m-v7m=7⇒v142=7⇒v=42×7=294 m s-1

From the given equations we can conclude that the phase difference between the waves is 3π i.e. odd multiple of π. Therefore, destructive interference will happen.

solution:
Each fork produces 4 beats per second with the previous means each fork has frequency 4 Hz more than the previous.

Using relation, flast=ffirst+N-1x, here, N is the number of tuning fork in series and x is beat frequency between two successive forks.

2f0=f0+ 20-14

⇒2f0=f0+76⇒f0=76 Hz

Frequency of last tuning fork is 152 Hz.

subject: Physics
solution:
The formula for the frequency in an open organ pipe is 

f=nv2L

Since, it is a second harmonic, n=2

The frequency of the second harmonic becomes f=2v2L=vL

The data given is 

L=0.4 mv=360 m s-1

Substituting the values in the frequency formula

f=360 m s-10.4 m=900 Hz

solution:
Speed of sound in a medium depends on inertial and elastic properties as $v=\sqrt{\frac{B}{P}}$ for gases and $v=\sqrt{\frac{Y}{P}}$ for solids. Since the elastic property of solid happens to be many folds greater than that of gases, the speed of sound in solids i5 higher than in gases.

Also, bulk modulus of gases varies between 0 and $\infty\left(B=-v \frac{d P}{d v}\right)$ hence reason is false.

subject: Physics
solution:
The equation of wave at any time is obtained by putting $\mathrm{X}=x-\mathrm{vt}$
$$
\mathrm{y}=\frac{1}{1+x^2}=\frac{1}{1+(x-v t)^2}
$$
We know at $t=2 \mathrm{sec}$,
$$
y=\frac{1}{1+(x-1)^2}
$$
On comparing (i) and (ii) we get $v t=1$
$$
V=\frac{1}{t}
$$
As $t=2 \mathrm{sec}$

$$
\therefore V=\frac{1}{2}=0.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \text {. }
$$

subject: Physics
solution:
$\mathrm{f}=500 \mathrm{~Hz}$
Case 1: When source is moving towards stationary listener apparent frequency $\eta^{\prime}=\eta\left(\frac{\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{v}-\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{s}}}\right)$ $$ =500\left(\frac{340}{336}\right)=506 \mathrm{~Hz} $$ Case 2 : When source is moving away from the stationary listener $$ \eta^{\prime \prime}=\eta\left(\frac{\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{v}+\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{s}}}\right)=500\left(\frac{340}{344}\right)=494 \mathrm{~Hz} $$ In case 1 number of beats heard is 6 and in case 2 number of beats heard is 18 therefore frequency of the source at $\mathrm{B}=512 \mathrm{~Hz}$
